articles.title: OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
articles.summary: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development. This acquisition underscores OpenAI's focus on proving their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

articles.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
articles.summary: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI. This tool addresses the increasing need for quality control as AI-generated code becomes more prevalent.

articles.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply-chain risk designation
articles.summary: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense after the agency labeled it a supply-chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful," representing a significant legal challenge to government AI regulation.

articles.title: Under the hood: Security architecture of GitHub Agentic Workflows
articles.summary: GitHub Agentic Workflows are built with isolation, constrained outputs, and comprehensive logging. The architecture includes threat modeling and security features to help teams run agents safely in GitHub Actions.

articles.title: Towards Autonomous Mathematics Research
articles.summary: Introduces Aletheia, a math research agent that iteratively generates, verifies, and revises solutions en route to professional mathematical research. The system bridges competition-level problem-solving to navigating vast literature and constructing long-horizon proofs.

articles.title: Accelerating Scientific Research with Gemini: Case Studies and Common Techniques
articles.summary: Presents case studies demonstrating how researchers have successfully collaborated with Google's Gemini-based models for novel mathematical discovery. The work explores the transition from routine task assistance to expert-level research contributions.

articles.title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
articles.summary: Introduces SAHOO, a practical framework to monitor and control alignment drift through the Goal Drift Index and constraint preservation mechanisms. The system addresses the risks of subtle alignment drift in recursive self-improvement systems.

articles.title: The Fragility Of Moral Judgment In Large Language Models
articles.summary: Introduces a perturbation framework for testing the stability and manipulability of LLM moral judgments while holding underlying moral conflicts constant. The study uses 2,939 dilemmas from r/AmItheAsshole to evaluate moral reasoning consistency.

articles.title: Real-Time AI Service Economy: A Framework for Agentic Computing Across the Continuum
articles.summary: Proposes a framework for real-time AI services operating across device-edge-cloud continuum where autonomous agents generate latency-sensitive workloads. The work addresses orchestration of multi-stage processing pipelines under resource and governance constraints.

articles.title: Employees across OpenAI and Google support Anthropic's lawsuit against the Pentagon
articles.summary: Nearly 40 employees from OpenAI and Google, including prominent figures like Jeff Dean, filed an amicus brief supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the Department of Defense. The brief details concerns over the Trump administration's decision to label Anthropic as a supply chain risk.

articles.title: Apple smart home display rumors now point to a fall launch with iOS 27
articles.summary: Apple's rumored smart home display has been delayed multiple times, with the latest reports pointing to a fall 2025 launch. The device was originally planned for 2025, then spring 2025, and now faces another delay while Apple works on the underlying software.

articles.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber will step aside
articles.summary: Jay Graber, who became CEO of Bluesky in 2021 when it spun out from Twitter, is stepping down from her role. Graber led the social network through its transition to independence and development of the AT Protocol.

articles.title: Apple's new M5 Max feels like a huge upgrade if you bought your laptop three years ago
articles.summary: Testing reveals the new M5 Max MacBooks show significant improvements over older models but only modest gains over M4 counterparts. The faster SSDs may be the primary reason to upgrade for users with newer laptops.

articles.title: Production query plans without production data
articles.summary: Discussion of techniques for analyzing database query performance without requiring access to sensitive production data.

articles.title: Perhaps not Boring Technology after all
articles.summary: Commentary on technology choices and the evolution of what constitutes "boring" versus innovative technology decisions.
